core_thesis.problem_statement: Systemic data friction exists between insurance carriers, restoration contractors, and mortgage lenders.
core_thesis.solution: An invisible cross-industry translation layer (Ontology Engine) that parses unstructured property insurance estimates (Xactimate PDFs) and automatically translates actuarial codes into operational CRM data and UAD 3.6 real estate appraisal standards.
core_thesis.market_arbitrage: Monetizing the 'ontological divide' between the ConTech silo (contractor operations) and the PropTech silo (mortgage compliance).

goas.name: Goal-Oriented Action System
goas.focus: Identifying and eliminating 'Human API' bottlenecks where highly paid humans manually translate unstructured data into structured assets, causing margin decay or capping revenue velocity.
constraints: Discard workflows where manual translation takes < 30 minutes.
Discard workflows lacking direct revenue restriction or margin destruction.
Do not clone existing software; build the invisible pipeline that renders the manual extraction process obsolete.

z_to_a_reverse_extrapolation.step_z: Define the final structured asset required for revenue or compliance.
z_to_a_reverse_extrapolation.step_y_x: Deduce the operational steps and mapping logic required to build the asset.
z_to_a_reverse_extrapolation.step_a: Pinpoint the exact chaotic intake format (unstructured PDFs, scans, emails).

competitive_landscape.conclusion: Verified White Space. Zero direct competitors bridging Xactimate parsing directly to UAD 3.6/MISMO 3.6 XML.

contech_silo_competitors.name: CapOut.ai
contech_silo_competitors.gap: Strictly contractor side; completely lacks appraisal compliance mapping (UAD 3.6).

contech_silo_competitors.name: EstimateHub / ClaimsFlow
contech_silo_competitors.gap: Focused only on Xactimate ecosystem imports; no cross-industry translation.

contech_silo_competitors.name: JobNimbus / AccuLynx
contech_silo_competitors.gap: Native integrations exist (e.g., via XactAnalysis), but still require structured intake. They are the destination, not the universal agnostic intake layer.

proptech_silo_competitors.name: ValueLink / Appraise-It Pro
proptech_silo_competitors.gap: Rely on traditional human appraiser workflow and manual data entry; do not ingest Xactimate PDFs or contractor material lists.

proptech_silo_competitors.name: Restb.ai
proptech_silo_competitors.gap: Guesses property condition based on photos; does not verify physical updates using contractor's exact material lists.

phase_1_wedge.name: Estimate-to-Operations Compiler
phase_1_wedge.step_z_goal: A fully structured job in the contractor’s CRM (contact, claim metadata, estimate structure, worksheet sections, production-ready material objects).
phase_1_wedge.human_api_pain: 45–90 minutes per estimate; $1,050–$3,600/month in direct labor burn at 40 packets/month.
phase_1_wedge.data_flow: Xactimate PDF Upload -> Parser extracts header/line items/totals -> Canonical claim graph structures data -> Adapter layer writes to JobNimbus/AccuLynx via API.

phase_2_moat.name: PropTech Compliance Bridge
phase_2_moat.step_z_goal: A compliance-ready UAD 3.6 / MISMO 3.6 XML file verifying property condition updates.
phase_2_moat.human_api_pain: 60–120 minutes of manual data entry per appraisal; caps daily revenue capacity.
phase_2_moat.data_flow: Ingest Phase 1 structured Xactimate data -> Algorithmically map granular construction line items to UAD 3.6 condition (C1-C6) and quality (Q1-Q6) codes -> Export MISMO 3.6 XML.

uad_mapping_logic_target.contech_output: Physical materials (e.g., Bundles, Nails, Ridge Cap) for CRM procurement
uad_mapping_logic_target.proptech_output: MISMO 3.6 XML Property Condition Rating (e.g., C1 - C6) and Quality Rating (e.g., Q1 - Q6)
